claim: Custom slash commands (prompt templates) stored in .claude/commands folder can accept dynamic variables and trigger complex multi-step workflows with a single command
actionable: Create prompt templates in .claude/commands with variable placeholders (e.g., {argument}) to reuse complex workflows; reference them as /command-name.
source_title: Claude Code's Most IMPORTANT FEATURE: Custom Slash Commands

claim: Context priming with a dedicated slash command resets Claude's understanding of the codebase in one step, eliminating the need to manually explore files every session
actionable: Build a /context-prime command that reads README and runs git ls-files to give Claude a complete codebase overview before tackling tasks.
source_title: Claude Code's Most IMPORTANT FEATURE: Custom Slash Commands

claim: Running multiple reasoning models in parallel (Claude, OpenAI, Gemini) on the same task and fusing results creates 3x compute for multi-perspective analysis without massive costs
actionable: Design prompt templates that invoke multiple LLM providers via MCP servers in parallel, then use fusion prompt chains to synthesize insights from all models.
source_title: Claude Code's Most IMPORTANT FEATURE: Custom Slash Commands

claim: Prompts are the new fundamental unit of knowledge work; control over prompts gives you control over compute and impact
actionable: Treat prompt engineering and prompt templates as core intellectual property; version and review them as carefully as you would production code.
source_title: Claude Code's Most IMPORTANT FEATURE: Custom Slash Commands

claim: Workflow tasks structured as high-level instructions with low-level detail sections (task name + task details) help Claude understand both goals and step-by-step execution
actionable: Write prompt templates with a concise high-level summary followed by detailed instructions for each step; this structure improves Claude's execution quality.
source_title: Claude Code's Most IMPORTANT FEATURE: Custom Slash Commands

claim: Fusion prompt chains (synthesizing outputs from multiple models using reasoning tokens) create comprehensive reviews that catch issues individual models miss
actionable: After running multiple models on a task, use Claude 3.7 Sonnet with thinking tokens to fuse and evaluate their outputs into a single, higher-quality result.
source_title: Claude Code's Most IMPORTANT FEATURE: Custom Slash Commands
